text.title: Gcp Overview Cli Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed when applying the gcp-overview-cli methodology. Each pairs a symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Downloaded SA key on dev machine = persistent project-wide access.
antipattern.root-cause: Engineer's `~/.config/gcloud` contains activated SA JSON key.
antipattern.fix: Switch to `gcloud auth application-default login` + impersonation; delete the SA key.

antipattern.symptom: Commands land in the wrong project after context switch.
antipattern.root-cause: Engineer relies on `gcloud config set project` instead of named configurations.
antipattern.fix: Create per-env `gcloud config configurations`; activate explicitly per task.

antipattern.symptom: Resources created in unexpected region after `config set` drift.
antipattern.fix: Pass `--region` and `--zone` explicitly on every command.

antipattern.symptom: Shell history syncs secrets to dotfiles or cloud sync.
antipattern.root-cause: `gcloud secrets versions add ... --data-file=- <<< 'plain-secret'` lands in history.
antipattern.fix: Use `set -o histignorespace` + leading space, or pass via file; rotate exposed secrets.
